parsed_review.summary: CP00-496 descriptor-only governance pack (RP16.P05.M05.S13-S22) passes independent read-only review: implementation/contract/tests/validator/command-evidence are fully consistent, no-write and no-real-data boundaries hold, Claude stays read-only and non-final-approver, Hermes is evidence-only, review hardening (invalid attempt-01 rejection, readiness preflight) is correctly applied, and production_ready is appropriately withheld. One trivial non-blocking metadata note; no P0/P1/P2 and no closeout blockers.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies new-session-handoff.md as both untracked implementation file and unrelated preserved file
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md is listed simultaneously in untracked_implementation_files (line 152) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 159). These two buckets are conceptually mutually exclusive, so the dual listing is internally inconsistent. The impact is nil for this pack: the authoritative active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty (and validation-report.json confirms active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted=[]), the file is not a CP00-496 required artifact (the pack's artifacts live under docs/closeout-packs/cp00-496/), and it is a session-handoff planning note rather than product/descriptor source. No review-scope gap results.
findings.recommendation: For metadata hygiene, classify docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md in exactly one bucket (it is a planning handoff doc, so unrelated_dirty_files_preserved is the better fit) so the scope artifact is unambiguous. No code, contract, test, or closeout change is required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking cosmetic metadata note; does not affect descriptor correctness, boundaries, tests, or closeout. Safe to defer or fix opportunistically. Does not block pack or goal closeout.
